name: Linear
one_liner: Linear is a project and issue tracking tool for software teams, exposing a comprehensive GraphQL API plus a TypeScript SDK for programmatic access to issues, projects, comments, cycles, and more.
access_notes: Any Linear account (including the free plan) can generate a personal API key from Settings > API without payment or approval, giving immediate working credentials. OAuth2 apps can also be created self-serve for building integrations that act on behalf of other users.
api_notes: Linear's public API is GraphQL-only (no REST). It is broad, covering issues, projects, teams, cycles, comments, labels, users, workflows, attachments, and more via queries and mutations. An official TypeScript SDK (@linear/sdk) wraps the GraphQL API with strongly typed models and operations.
mcp_notes: Linear ships an official remote MCP server hosted at https://mcp.linear.app/mcp (announced in their May 2025 changelog and documented at linear.app/docs/mcp). It supports finding, creating, and updating issues, projects, and comments, and connects natively in Claude, Cursor, and other MCP clients. Numerous community MCP servers also exist.
main_blocker: None significant. API is public and credentials are self-serve/free. Only minor consideration is that the API is GraphQL-only, so integrations must speak GraphQL rather than REST.

evidence.claim: Linear offers a GraphQL API with OAuth2 and personal API key authentication, plus a TypeScript SDK and webhooks.
Linear ships an official remote MCP server at https://mcp.linear.app/mcp with tools to find, create, and update issues, projects, and comments.
Linear announced its official MCP server in the May 2025 changelog.

open_questions: Exact rate limits and whether any advanced API features (e.g., certain webhook resource types or OAuth actor authorization) require a paid plan were not fully verified against the pricing page.
